metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the Gerrit reader's artifact-lookup algorithm: state whether it does a title-prefix scan for `code-review: {change_id} @` and picks the most-recent result, uses a change-level pointer artifact, or some other mechanism.
findings.finding: The Gerrit reader key is described as 'the Gerrit change' but existing artifact titles include the revision (`code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}`). An executor cannot determine without clarification how the reader locates the most-recent prior-revision artifact for a new patchset: by title-prefix scan, by explicit revision lookup, or another mechanism.
findings.location: Design section, 'Keying + addressing' paragraph: 'Gerrit reviews are keyed on the Gerrit change' vs. 'Gerrit uses "code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}" (existing)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence: 'The Gerrit reader performs a title-prefix scan for `code-review: {change_id} @`, sorts results by artifact creation timestamp descending, and picks the first match as the prior-run artifact; if no match exists, it returns an empty prior set (REGION_UNKNOWN for all files).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split AC8 into two parts: (a) this epic's scoped deliverable ('c639 makes the code-review reader filter to surfaced-only findings, verified by a unit test'), and (b) a cross-epic dependency note ('full gate agreement requires old-frilly-plankton; track separately').
findings.finding: AC8 claims a durable end-state that 'both gates agree' (code-review reader and plan-review novelty prior set are both surfaced-only), but the plan-review half of this end-state is owned by a separate child epic (old-frilly-plankton). The AC as written cannot be verified as complete within this epic's session alone.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC8: 'The shared-definition fix is owned end-to-end: c639 (this epic) makes the code-review reader surfaced-only, and bug old-frilly-plankton (a child of this epic) makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only, so both gates agree.'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC8 as: 'The code-review reader loads only findings with decision in {block, advisory}, verified by a unit test asserting dropped findings are excluded from the prior set. (Full cross-gate agreement with plan-review depends on old-frilly-plankton and is tracked there.)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Qualify SC1 to apply only when a persistent session identity exists (e.g. 'On a re-run within the same session lifecycle...'), or add a note cross-referencing the uuid-fallback tradeoff so the observable proxy is unambiguous.
findings.finding: Success Criteria SC1 states 'On a re-run of a review with NO code changes to the cited files, every prior low-priority SURFACED finding is labeled carried_over' as an unqualified claim, but the Design section explicitly states local convergence is 'intentionally INERT' for bare `rebar review-code` invocations with no session lifecycle (uuid fallback). The SC does not scope itself to 'session-lifecycle-present' runs, so an implementer testing SC1 against plain `rebar review-code` invocations would encounter a contradiction between promised behavior and the designed fallback.
findings.location: Success Criteria (SC1) vs. Design section (uuid-fallback rationale paragraph)
findings.suggested_fix: Amend SC1 to read: 'On a re-run of a review within a persistent session (session lifecycle present) with NO code changes to the cited files, every prior low-priority SURFACED finding is labeled carried_over and NOT re-coached... (Note: bare `rebar review-code` with no session lifecycle uses a per-invocation uuid, so convergence is intentionally inert in that mode — see uuid-fallback rationale.)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC verifying that `code_review_novelty.md` exists, uses a diff+finding-list template (not `{{plan}}`), and is registered under the `code-novelty` reviewer id, so this primary deliverable has an explicit acceptance gate.
findings.finding: The Design section describes two concrete deliverables — a new reviewer prompt file `code_review_novelty.md` and a new reviewer id `code-novelty` — that have no covering Acceptance Criterion. These are non-trivial artifacts (the prompt drives all novelty scoring for code review) but zero ACs verify their existence, content, or correct wiring.
findings.location: Design section (REUSE boundary paragraph) vs. Acceptance Criteria (AC1–AC8)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Design's internal cross-reference from '(see AC5 / old-frilly-plankton)' to '(see AC1 / AC8 / old-frilly-plankton)' so it points to the ACs that actually gate the surfaced-only novelty-prior invariant.
findings.finding: The Design section cross-references 'AC5' to mean 'novelty scored only against surfaced findings,' but actual AC5 in the Acceptance Criteria is about carryover labeling (carried_from field, skipped in coaching). The internal reference is wrong, creating terminology drift between what the Design claims AC5 covers and what AC5 actually says.
findings.location: Design section (Novelty paragraph: 'never against dropped/hidden findings (see AC5 / old-frilly-plankton)') vs. Acceptance Criteria AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'never against dropped/hidden findings (see AC5 / old-frilly-plankton)' to 'never against dropped/hidden findings (see AC1, AC8 / old-frilly-plankton)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge the `code_review_result_v1` schema evolution explicitly: state whether it is additive-only (and that all consumers tolerate unknown fields), or add a schema version bump + expand-contract sequence + rollback path for any consumer that validates the schema.
findings.finding: The plan adds new fields (`session_id`, `{path: sha256}` deps map, `carried_from`) to the existing `code_review_result_v1` artifact payload schema without acknowledging the schema evolution, specifying a version bump, or providing a rollback/back-out plan for consumers that validate the schema strictly.
findings.location: AC2: 'The code_review_result_v1 payload carries session_id (top-level) and a {path: sha256} deps map'; Design: 'Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence confirming all existing readers of code_review_result_v1 use loose/additive parsing (ignore unknown fields), OR specify a schema version field increment and an expand-contract migration step (write v1 compat fields first, then cut readers over). Document the rollback: if the new fields cause a consumer failure, the artifact writer can be reverted independently of the reader.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update ADR 0008 (or create a companion ADR/design note) to document the code-review novelty gate extension: the region-gate vocabulary, the cross-gate kernel reuse contract, and the deliberate decision against a shared novelty-engine abstraction.
findings.finding: The plan extends ADR 0008's novelty/rising-floor mechanism to a new gate (code review, with region gating) — a significant new architectural decision — but does not mention updating ADR 0008 or creating a new ADR to document the code-review novelty gate pattern, the region-gate vocabulary, or the cross-gate reuse contract.
findings.location: Design section: 'REUSE boundary is the contract, not the prompt. Code review calls TWO pure kernel primitives UNCHANGED'; references to ADR 0008
findings.suggested_fix: Add an 'Extensions' section to ADR 0008 (or a new ADR 0009) covering: (1) the region_gated_floor pattern and REGION_* vocabulary, (2) the gate-specific prompt convention (code_review_novelty.md alongside plan_review_novelty.md), (3) the rationale for no shared abstraction. Reference this from the epic plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update `docs/session-id-shims.md` to document that REBAR_SESSION_ID now also gates code-review cross-run convergence, including the uuid-fallback behavior (inert convergence) and how to opt into genuine convergence via a stable session ID.
findings.finding: The plan references `docs/session-id-shims.md` as already documenting the SessionStart shim pattern, but does not mention updating that doc to cover the new consequence of session ID presence/absence: whether code-review convergence is active or inert. Users of the shim doc would not learn that their session configuration now also gates cross-run code-review noise reduction.
findings.location: Design section: 'a SessionStart shim exporting REBAR_SESSION_ID (docs/session-id-shims.md) for harnesses lacking a native var'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Code-review convergence' subsection to docs/session-id-shims.md explaining: (1) with a stable session ID, repeated local reviews converge (noise reduced); (2) without one, each invocation is isolated (uuid fallback, inert convergence); (3) cite the uuid-fallback rationale from this epic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a prompt-quality validation step for code_review_novelty.md: a small eval fixture (e.g., 5–10 labeled finding pairs with known novelty) run against the real LLM to confirm the prompt discriminates novel vs. repeated code findings before the gate goes live.
findings.finding: The new code_review_novelty.md prompt — which scores code findings for novelty against a diff+finding-list — is asserted to work correctly but has no empirical validation step (pilot, eval fixture, or sample scoring run). The test harness (AC3) exercises the predicate logic but presumably mocks the LLM call, leaving the prompt's actual discrimination quality unmeasured before build-out.
findings.location: Design (settled) — 'code review adds its own novelty reviewer prompt (code_review_novelty.md, oriented to a diff + a finding list)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or task to create/update an ADR (or CLAUDE.md section) capturing the per-gate novelty seam contract, region gate design, content-hash proxy rationale, typed keyspace isolation, and uuid-fallback tradeoff — so the pattern is discoverable independent of the epic ticket.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural decision — per-gate novelty prompts, region-gated floor, and content-hash-based change detection as a code-review-specific convergence mechanism — but does not call for a new ADR or update to CLAUDE.md / design docs. ADR 0008 covers the plan-review rising-floor; the extension to code review with a region gate is a distinct design decision that future maintainers need documented.
findings.location: Design (settled) — overall; Success Criteria; no ADR creation mentioned anywhere in the plan
findings.suggested_fix: Create a new ADR (e.g., ADR 00XX: Code-Review Cross-Patchset Novelty Gate) that documents: (1) per-gate prompt contract, (2) REGION_* state machine and file-level proxy rationale, (3) typed keyspace isolation design, (4) uuid-fallback conscious tradeoff, (5) surfaced-only prior set invariant. Reference it from ADR 0008.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one workflow-level validation mechanism (e.g., a dogfooding step: run `rebar review-code` twice on an unchanged change and confirm the second run surfaces fewer advisory findings, or define a before/after metric target) so the primary value claim is end-to-end verifiable, not only unit-test-verifiable.
findings.finding: The plan's validation mechanism for its primary value claim ('repeat-noise reduced') is entirely a unit-test harness (AC3: 'verifiable by the region_gated_floor test harness'), with no workflow-level before/after comparison, operational metric, or dogfooding scenario described. The criterion requires at least one concrete validation mechanism at the user/workflow level.
findings.location: Success Criteria (first bullet) and AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Success Criterion: 'In a dogfooding run, a second `rebar review-code` invocation on a Gerrit change with no code edits surfaces zero previously-surfaced low-priority findings (confirmed by comparing the two run artifacts), demonstrating end-to-end noise reduction.' This complements the unit-test harness without replacing it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] (Advisory) Confirm that parenting old-frilly-plankton under the code-review epic is intentional ownership rather than a filing convenience — if plan-review has its own epic or backlog, consider re-parenting it there and referencing it from AC8 as a dependency instead.
findings.finding: old-frilly-plankton (plan-review novelty floor bug fix) is parented to a code-review epic yet touches a separate subsystem (plan_review) with its own independent reason-to-change (plan-review correctness). This introduces mild cross-subsystem diffusion: if plan-review's novelty floor needs further fixes in the future, the genealogy would tie them back to a code-review epic rather than a plan-review one.
findings.location: Child ticket old-frilly-plankton; AC8: 'the shared-definition fix is owned end-to-end'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify WHERE and HOW the carried_from coaching skip is wired (e.g., a pre-coach filter in the code-review workflow that strips carried_from findings from the `surviving` list before passing to the kernel `coach()`, keeping the kernel gate-agnostic).
findings.finding: The plan specifies that carryover findings are 'OMITTED from coaching; they remain surfaced' (AC5), but does not specify the mechanism by which Pass-4's coach() skips carried_from findings. The existing kernel coach() takes a `surviving` list and runs the move-pick LLM call over all of them; there is no existing `carried_from`-aware filter path. The implementation must either (a) strip carried_from findings before passing to coach(), (b) add a flag check inside coach(), or (c) pass them through coach() but suppress their coaching output at render time — and these have different blast-radius implications (option a changes what coach() sees; option b changes the kernel). The plan leaves this underspecified.
findings.location: AC5 / Design section ('Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from ... skipped in coaching')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the Design section: 'The coaching skip for carryover findings is applied as a pre-coach filter in the code-review workflow (produce_code_review_verdict / region_gated_floor post-processing): carried_from findings are removed from the list passed to coach() but re-inserted into advisory in the final verdict. This keeps the kernel coach() gate-agnostic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether the diff text is included in the code_review_novelty.md system prompt (and if so, how it is truncated/summarized when large) or confirm that file-level REGION_UNCHANGED already handles this case before the novelty sub-call fires.
findings.finding: The plan's system-prompt contract for code_review_novelty.md is under-specified relative to the existing plan_review_novelty.md pattern. The plan says the new prompt is 'oriented to a diff + a finding list' but does not state what grounding context (analogous to {{plan}}) is embedded in the system prompt. In plan-review, the system prompt includes the plan text via `passes._resolve_system(passes.PASS_NOVELTY, ctx.plan_text, vcfg)` so the LLM can re-ground the matches-prior judgment. For code review, the analogous grounding is the diff text — but the diff can be large, and `score_novelty` already passes prior findings as the `context` argument. If the diff is not included in the system prompt, the LLM cannot re-ground whether 'the same line' was flagged before vs. now on a changed diff, weakening the matches-prior accuracy for code findings (which are location-anchored, unlike plan findings).
findings.location: Design section ('code_review_novelty.md, oriented to a diff + a finding list')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief rationale in the Design section explaining why the region gate on the *drop predicate* (Pass-3 only, with Pass-1 still running fully) is architecturally distinct from — and not contradicted by — ADR 0008's rejection of per-diff-region scoping for Pass-1 recall.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a file-level region gate (REGION_UNCHANGED conjunct in `region_gated_floor`) as the key novel element, but ADR 0008 — explicitly cited in the plan as the blueprint — formally rejected 'per-diff-region scoping' in its Alternatives Rejected section. The plan does not distinguish why the region gate on the *drop predicate* is architecturally sound while ADR 0008's rejection target (region scoping for *Pass-1 recall*) is not — leaving an apparent contradiction in the project's own ADR record that implementers and future reviewers will encounter.
findings.location: Design section — 'Region gate is content-addressed and resilient'; ADR 0008 Alternatives Rejected: 'Per-diff-region scoping'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical or note to the Design's 'region gate' paragraph: 'Note: ADR 0008 rejected per-diff-region scoping for Pass-1 recall (it would miss defects outside the diffed region). The region gate here is applied at Pass-3 drop-only: Pass-1 always runs the full criteria set; the region state merely controls whether a novel low-priority finding is dropped. Full recall is preserved.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify in the Design (and AC1/AC6) that when a prior artifact is structurally valid but lacks the `{path: sha256}` deps map field (pre-migration artifact), the reader treats all file regions as REGION_UNKNOWN — not REGION_CHANGED and not an error — so old artifacts fall through the safe path without relying on the exception-based fail-safe.
findings.finding: The plan adds new fields to the `code_review_result_v1` persisted schema (`session_id`, `{path: sha256}` deps map, `carried_from`) but does not explicitly specify reader behavior for pre-existing artifacts that lack these fields. The fail-safe is described as handling *errors* (exceptions during load/hash/compare), but a structurally valid old artifact with absent fields is not an error — it is a known, expected steady-state. If the reader silently treats a missing deps map as an empty dict it may compute REGION_CHANGED (no prior hash) rather than REGION_UNKNOWN, producing a different gating outcome than the fail-safe path.
findings.location: Design section — 'region gate is content-addressed' paragraph; AC1 (reader loads prior findings + content-hash map); AC6 (fail-safe)
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Design's region-gate paragraph: 'A prior artifact with no deps map field (old format) is treated as if every file region is REGION_UNKNOWN — no drops are applied, and this is NOT an error condition (no try/except needed for this branch).' Mirror this in AC1: 'the reader treats absent deps map as all-REGION_UNKNOWN.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify `code_review_novelty.md`'s required structural metadata: the template variable that injects diff context (replacing `{{plan}}`), the `outputs:` schema name, `execution_mode`, and whether a `code_review_novelty` contract must be registered in `code_review/contracts.py` (analogously to `plan_review/passes.py:208`).
findings.finding: The plan mandates a new `code_review_novelty.md` reviewer prompt and `code-novelty` reviewer id, but does not specify the prompt's required structural metadata: the template variable that replaces `{{plan}}` (used in `plan_review_novelty.md` to inject plan text — code review uses a diff, not a plan), the `outputs:` schema name (which must match a registered contract), the `execution_mode`, or whether a new `code_review_novelty` contract must be registered in `code_review/contracts.py`. Without these, an implementer cannot wire the sub-call analogously to the `plan-novelty` path.
findings.location: Design section — 'code review adds its own novelty reviewer prompt (code_review_novelty.md, oriented to a diff + a finding list) and a new reviewer id (code-novelty)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Design section: 'code_review_novelty.md declares `outputs: code_review_novelty` (the same NoveltyOutput model, registered under a new key in code_review/contracts.py), `execution_mode: single_turn`, and uses a `{{diff}}` template variable to inject the current diff as context (replacing plan_review_novelty.md's `{{plan}}`). The reviewer id `code-novelty` is added to reviewers/index.json.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `cites_prior_location` in `code_review_novelty.md` is re-described for file/line semantics ('same file path and line range as that prior finding') or whether a separate code-scoped sub-answer tuple is introduced, so the prompt vocabulary matches the code-review citation format.
findings.finding: The plan reuses `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` (`restates_prior_defect`, `cites_prior_location`, `matches_prior_fix`) without specifying how the code-review novelty prompt should answer `cites_prior_location`, whose existing definition is explicitly 'same plan location/section as that prior finding' — a plan-domain concept — whereas code-review citations are file/line pairs.
findings.location: Design section — 'REUSE boundary is the contract, not the prompt'; AC4 — 'region_gated_floor reuses the novelty sub-call'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly add `"novelty-region"` to the `drop_reason` vocabulary (currently `{null, "novelty", "completion"}` per ADR 0024) and note that the ADR's vocabulary table must be updated as part of this epic's deliverables.
findings.finding: The plan introduces `drop_reason: "novelty-region"` as a new dropped-finding verdict value, but the existing `drop_reason` vocabulary is a documented enum (`{null, "novelty", "completion"}` per ADR 0024 and sidecar.py) and the plan never acknowledges extending it. Downstream consumers (calibration corpus readers, the sidecar join, offline analytics) that switch on `drop_reason` will encounter an undocumented value.
findings.location: Design section — 'Dropped novel-on-unchanged findings move to the verdict dropped bucket with drop_reason "novelty-region"'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Design section: 'The drop_reason vocabulary (ADR 0024) is extended with a fourth value "novelty-region" (dropped: novel + low-priority + REGION_UNCHANGED). ADR 0024 section "The sidecar drop_reason field" is updated as a deliverable of this epic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the type and value format of `carried_from` (e.g., `str` holding the artifact title key such as `'code-review: session:{session_id}'` or `'code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}'`) so the field has a deterministic, testable value across both local and Gerrit paths.
findings.finding: The `carried_from` schema field is introduced without a type definition. The plan says it carries 'the prior run/patchset key' but does not specify whether it is a string (the artifact title like 'code-review: session:abc'), a structured dict, or the typed key value (e.g. 'session:abc' or 'change:123'). This is a schema field on every carryover finding and is referenced by AC5 ('labeled via the carried_from field').
findings.location: Design section — 'Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from (the prior run/patchset key)'; AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Design section: 'carried_from is a str holding the prior artifact's title key (the same string used to address the artifact: "code-review: session:{session_id}" for local, "code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}" for Gerrit).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] 3bff must explicitly specify that the local code path sets `target_ticket` on `CodeReviewRequest` (or provides an alternate emit route) so the `if request.target_ticket:` guard in gate_dispatch fires for local reviews.
findings.finding: child 3bff's local emit path depends on the target_ticket gate in gate_dispatch._run_code_review_gate, but the current local shim.py creates CodeReviewRequest with no target_ticket — the existing emit is gated on `if request.target_ticket:` (gate_dispatch.py:592) and the shim never sets it. 3bff's scope lists shim.py and _llm_commands.py but its AC1 only says 'emits a code_review artifact keyed session:<id>' without specifying that the local path must set target_ticket on CodeReviewRequest (or take an alternate emit path).
findings.location: child 3bff-cdf6-8fca-4c33, AC1 / shim.py review_code() → CodeReviewRequest(target_ticket=None)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 3bff's AC or What section: 'Wire `target_ticket` on `CodeReviewRequest` for local runs (the artifact ticket id resolved or created by the session-keyed emit path), so the existing `if request.target_ticket:` guard in `_run_code_review_gate` fires.' Alternatively, document the alternate emit route if one is chosen.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] c639 must specify the exact injection mechanism for deps (e.g., injected into the verdict dict in produce_code_review_verdict so both emits carry it), and address the double-emit scenario for the Gerrit voter path.
findings.finding: c639 states 'The deps map is computed once in the gate and reaches both local and Gerrit emits via the existing emit,' but the Gerrit voter (voter.py emit_code_review_artifact) calls sidecar.emit SEPARATELY and INDEPENDENTLY from gate_dispatch's sidecar emit — both using the same verdict dict. The mechanism by which deps reaches the voter's separate emit is unspecified: if deps is injected into the verdict dict by produce_code_review_verdict, the voter's subsequent emit call would include it; but this injection point and the resulting double-emit are not addressed in c639.
findings.location: child c639-4122-58d9-4dc2, AC5 / voter.py emit_code_review_artifact vs gate_dispatch._run_code_review_gate
findings.suggested_fix: Specify in c639's What/AC that deps is injected into the verdict dict (e.g., verdict['deps'] = computed_deps) inside produce_code_review_verdict before either sidecar.emit is called, ensuring both the gate_dispatch emit and the voter's independent emit carry deps via build_payload reading verdict['deps'].

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] c3e4 must close the 'decide where filter lives' question with an explicit outcome committed to a specific AC, not just an open 'decide' item — and if the decision affects latest_review_result's signature, it must be reconciled with c639's analogous reader design.
findings.finding: child c3e4's third AC item ('Audit every novelty prior-set consumer (attest.py:441 also calls latest_review_result) to confirm none feeds dropped findings as novelty priors') and fourth AC ('Decide whether the surfaced-only filter belongs in latest_review_result vs each caller') together form a partial-without-remainder pattern: the audit is in scope but the decision outcome is explicitly deferred. If the decision is 'filter in latest_review_result,' it affects c639 (which is designing its own analogous reader) and possibly the attest.py usage. Neither outcome option is assigned to a specific child.
findings.location: child c3e4-7a6c-a4cc-4fb8, AC3/AC4 / plan_review/attest.py:441
findings.suggested_fix: Add a committed outcome to c3e4 AC4, e.g.: 'Filter in _maybe_apply_rising_floor (each caller), not in latest_review_result, to preserve the function's existing contract for attest.py usage; document this as the pattern for both gates.' Then verify this matches c639's approach.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] e51b should clarify that 'both paths' means both local and Gerrit flow through the same gate workflow (not that voter.py needs a separate hook), and confirm voter.py is not in scope.
findings.finding: e51b's What section says 'Wire into BOTH gate paths (local + Gerrit voter)' but its Scope says 'src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py (decide + coach seams), new region_gate.py, new code_review_novelty.md reviewer prompt, reuse of review_kernel' — the Gerrit voter path (src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py) is not in e51b's scope. The region_gated_floor must run INSIDE produce_code_review_verdict (before the verdict reaches the voter) to gate both paths; if it runs only after decide and before coach in the workflow, it would cover both local and Gerrit (since both use the same gate). But the inconsistency between the 'both paths' claim and the scope listing that omits voter.py is an unresolved ambiguity about the insertion point.
findings.location: child e51b-bf7c-63e0-4d06, What vs Scope sections
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to e51b's What: 'Both local and Gerrit reviews run through produce_code_review_verdict → the gate workflow, so wiring region_gated_floor into workflow_ops.py (between the decide and coach seams) covers both paths without touching voter.py.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify implementation order (c639 before 3bff, since 3bff depends on c639's payload shape) or define a minimum stub contract 3bff can emit pending c639's build_payload extension.
findings.finding: The 3bff child's ordering dependency on c639 is stated as 'Depends on the payload shape from the reader/payload story' but is not marked as a blocking prerequisite or given an explicit sequencing constraint. The 3bff child's own AC1 says the artifact carries 'the reviewed-file deps map,' which is defined and computed by c639. If 3bff is implemented first, its emit would produce artifacts without the deps field, which is both incomplete and inconsistent with the schema c639 defines. No child specifies the implementation order or a minimum stub contract 3bff can emit before c639 lands.
findings.location: child 3bff-cdf6-8fca-4c33, Depends on / child c639-4122-58d9-4dc2
findings.suggested_fix: Mark c639 as a hard prerequisite for 3bff (not just a soft dependency), or move the build_payload session_id + deps extension into 3bff's scope with c639 adding only the reader.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
